leadText: The King's Rock (Japanese: おうじゃのしるし King's Symbol) is a type of held item introduced in Generation II. It allows Poliwhirl and Slowpoke to evolve into Politoed and Slowking, respectively, and also allows the holder's moves to cause Pokémon to flinch.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation II 1.1.2 Generations III and IV 1.1.3 Generation V onward 1.1.4 Moves affected before Generation V 1.2 Outside battles Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Grants certain moves used by the holder a chance of causing targets to flinch . Generation II [ edit | edit source ] Grants the moves listed below a 11.71875% (30/256) chance of causing targets to flinch. Unlike flinching caused by other means in this generation, King's Rock can cause sleeping and frozen Pokémon to flinch as well. Only the final strike of a multistrike move has a chance of causing the target to flinch. Generations III and IV [ edit | edit source ] Grants the moves listed below a 10% chance of causing each target to flinch. This chance is not affected by Serene Grace . Each strike of a multistrike move has an independent chance of causing the target to flinch. Flinching caused this way is no longer different to flinching caused by other means. Due to how this item's effect is resolved in Generation IV, U-turn will be able to make the target flinch only if the Pokémon replacing the user is holding the item. Generation V onward [ edit | edit source ] Grants all attacking moves that do not already have a chance to flinch a 10% chance of causing each target to flinch. This chance is affected by Serene Grace and the rainbow created by Fire Pledge and Water Pledge . King's Rock does not activate Sheer Force , but Sheer Force removes the chance to flinch if the move already has an additional effect . edit source ] Causes Poliwhirl to evolve into Politoed and Slowpoke to evolve into Slowking when traded while holding it; this consumes the King's Rock. They will not evolve if linking to a Generation I game.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] If the player offers both a Link Cable and King's Rock at Luminous Cave or Luminous Spring , Slowpoke or Poliwhirl can be evolved into Slowking or Politoed , respectively. This consumes the Link Cable and King's Rock. If thrown, it will deal 1 damage (2 if sticky ). Using this item in a dungeon will consume it, with no effect.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] The King's Rock is required to evolve certain Pokémon in Pokémon GO. It is consumed upon use. Poliwhirl requires 100 Poliwag Candy and a King's Rock to evolve into Politoed . Slowpoke requires 50 Slowpoke Candy and a King's Rock to evolve into Slowking .

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] The King's Rock is required to evolve certain Pokémon in Pokémon Sleep. It is consumed upon use. Slowpoke requires 80 Slowpoke Candy , a King's Rock, and a Linking Cord to evolve into Slowking .
